title: PROCEDURAL BASIS OF CONSIDERING A CRIMINAL CASE IN THE PROCEDURE OF REVIEW
author: Nazarov Zafar Safarboyevich
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: this article covers the procedural foundations of reviewing criminal cases in the review procedure, the powers of the review body, and what should be paid attention to when reviewing cases in terms of substance. In addition, the grounds for canceling or changing the verdicts of first instance courts are studied, and the structure of court decisions adopted by higher courts is considered.
steps_raw: this article covers the procedural foundations of reviewing criminal cases in the review procedure, the powers of the review body, and what should be paid attention to when reviewing cases in terms of substance. In addition, the grounds for canceling or changing the verdicts of first instance courts are studied, and the structure of court decisions adopted by higher courts is considered.
